popularStory.description:
BIO:  from Historic Peerage of England, p 337
...1303 summoned to Parliament from 22 Feb. 34 Edw. I. 1306, to 3 Dec. 20 Edw. II. 1326, with the addition of "de Wigmore;" created Earl of March 9 Nov. 1 …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March於1287年4月25日出生在英國賀福爾維格莫 卡素時，他的父親Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer34歲，母親Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes38歲。他於1301年9月20日與Joan de Geneville在英國賀福爾布利其結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和10個女兒的父母。他於1316年在愛爾蘭被列為lord lieutenant of ireland。他去世於1330年11月29日在英國倫敦享年43歲，埋葬在英國賀福爾維格莫。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March于1287年4月25日出生在英国賀福爾維格莫 卡素时，他的父亲Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer34岁，母亲Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes38岁。他于1301年9月20日与Joan de Geneville在英国賀福爾布利其结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和10个女儿的父母。他于1316年在爱尔兰被列为lord lieutenant of ireland。他去世于1330年11月29日在英国倫敦享年43岁，埋葬在英国賀福爾維格莫。

nameMeanings.name: Mortimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mortemer in Seine-Maritime, France, so called from Old French mort(e) ‘dead’ + mer ‘sea’, probably referring to a stagnant pond or drained swamp.

Irish (of Norman origin): the Norman surname in sense 1 above was taken to Ireland from England in the medieval period, where it has also been adopted by bearers of the Gaelic surnames Mac Muircheartaigh and ÓMuircheartaigh, commonly Anglicized as McMurty and Mortagh. Compare McMurdo .

variants.name: Mortimore
meanings: English (Devon; of Norman origin): variant of   Mortimer  .

variants.name: McMurdo
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muircheartaigh or Mac Muircheartaich, ‘son of Muircheartach’, a personal name meaning ‘sea-ruler’. In Scotland the name was often lenited to give McCurdy . Compare Murdoch and Moriarty . The name was also used to Anglicize Mac Murchaidh (see McMorrow ).

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March was born on 25 April 1287, in Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, England, his father, Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer, was 34 and his mother, Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes, was 38. He married Joan de Geneville on 20 September 1301, in Pembridge, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 10 daughters. In 1316, at the age of 29, his occupation is listed as lord lieutenant of ireland in Ireland. He died on 29 November 1330, in London, England, at the age of 43, and was buried in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England.

fr.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, City of: Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March est né le 25 avril 1287, à Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Joan de Geneville le 20 septembre 1301, à Pembridge, Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 10 filles. En 1316, à l’âge de 29 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est lord lieutenant of ireland à Irlande. Il est décédé le 29 novembre 1330, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Wigmore, Herefordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March am 25. April 1287 in Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer 34 und seine Mutter Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes 38. Er hat am 20. September 1301 in Pembridge, Herefordshire, England Joan de Geneville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 10 Töchter. 1316, als er 29 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als lord lieutenant of ireland in Irland aufgeführt. Er starb am 29. November 1330 im Alter von 43 Jahren in London, England und wurde in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March è nato il il 25 aprile 1287 nel Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Joan de Geneville il 20 settembre 1301, nel Pembridge, Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 10 figlie. Nel 1316, all'età di 29 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come lord lieutenant of ireland nel Irlanda. Morì il 29 novembre 1330, nel London, England, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Wigmore, Herefordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of Marchが1287年4月25日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモー カストルで生まれた時，父Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimerは34歳，母Margaret Eleanor de Fiennesは38歳であった。1301年9月20日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアペンブリッジでJoan de Genevilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と10人の娘の親であった。1316年，29歳の時，アイルランドでの職業はlord lieutenant of irelandと記録されている。彼は1330年11月29日にイギリスロンドンで43歳で死去し，イギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March(이)가 1287년 4월 25일 영국 허퍼드실르 위그모어 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1301년 9월 20일 영국 허퍼드실르 p브리지에서 Joan de Geneville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 1316년, 29세의 나이로 그의 직업은 아일랜드에서 lord lieutenant of ireland이었다. 그는 1330년 11월 29일 영국 런던에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허퍼드실르 위그모어에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March nasceu em 25 de abril de 1287, em Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan de Geneville em 20 de setembro de 1301, em Pembridge, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 10 filhas. Em 1316, com 29 anos, sua profissão aparece como lord lieutenant of ireland em Irlanda. Ele faleceu em 29 de novembro de 1330, em London, Inglaterra, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, City of: вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March родился 25 апреля 1287 г. в местности вигмор кастл, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Geneville 20 сентября 1301 г. в местности пембридж, хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 10 дочерей. В 1316 году, в возрасте 29 лет,  он работал по профессии lord lieutenant of ireland в местности Ирландия. Он умер 29 ноября 1330 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности вигмор, хирефордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March nació el 25 de abril de 1287, en Wigmore Castle, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Edmund Mortimer 2nd Lord Mortimer, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Margaret Eleanor de Fiennes, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Joan de Geneville el 20 de septiembre de 1301, en Pembridge, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 10 hijas. En 1316, a la edad de 29 años, su ocupación registrada era lord lieutenant of ireland en Irlanda. Murió el 29 de noviembre de 1330, en London, Inglaterra, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.
